_meta.description: Engineering augmentations layered on top of the LLM-extracted playbook content. This file contains ONLY decisions that are NOT in the SeeWeeS playbook; everything else flows from extracted_context.json.
_meta.rationale: We deliberately keep these out of the LLM extraction step to avoid hallucination. They are reviewed by humans and version-controlled.

C1_I95_NJ_BOS.rationale: Playbook §7 ties tier to medicine category. For v1 we simplify: C1 carries primarily life-critical Boston oncology/MGH traffic so is treated as Tier 1 (6h cap). base_transit_hours estimated from highway distance (NJ->BOS ~ 220 mi at average truck speed).

C2_NJ_PHL.rationale: C2 carries standard specialty traffic so is treated as Tier 2 (12h cap). base_transit_hours estimated from highway distance (NJ->PHL ~ 90 mi).

truck_augmentations.rationale: Playbook §8: 10 capacity / 1.10 packing buffer = 9.09, floored to 9 to stay conservative.

cold_chain_rule.definition: Any item whose temp_control field is NOT 'Room Temp (20-25C)' is treated as cold-chain and requires truck_temp_controlled.
cold_chain_rule.rationale: Playbook §9 (Medicine Master Reference) describes temp_control verbatim; this rule is the operational interpretation.

data_quality_config.rationale: Playbook Appendix A.5/A.6 D1 allow regex-based regeneration of missing unique_item_id values. We enable this so flagged units can still be dispatched. Generated UIDs use a clearly-marked placeholder format (RMD-2026-GEN001, INS-2026-GEN001, etc.) so they can never be confused with real inventory IDs.
